title: Super Nintendo Platform Stack
body:
# Super Nintendo Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `console_snes` · **Era:** 1990–1996 · **Vendor:** Nintendo · **Count:** 10

Ricoh **5A22** (65C816 @ 3.58 MHz), dual PPU, Sony **SPC700** audio sub-system — launched **November 1990** in Japan.

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: console_snes` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **Ricoh 5A22 CPU Glue** (Ricoh, 16-bit) — 65C816 derivative — SNES main CPU bus + DMA glue
- **Nintendo SA-1 Coprocessor** (Nintendo, 16-bit) — SA-1 enhancement ASIC — BW-RAM arithmetic copro
- **Sony S-SMP (SPC700)** (Sony, 8-bit) — SPC700 audio sub-CPU — 64KB ARAM + DSP
- **SNES PPU (S-PPU)** (Nintendo, 16-bit) — Picture Processing Unit — dual 8-bit BG layers
- **SNES CIC Lockout** (Nintendo, 8-bit) — Region lock + reset handshake — cart/console glue
- **SNES WRAM Controller** (Nintendo, 16-bit) — Work RAM bus arbiter — CPU/PPU/DMA access

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **10** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: console_snes · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
